title: The Synapse Telecom Data Exfiltration
summary: During the night of June 18, 2025, Synapse Telecom detected an unauthorized extraction of sensitive internal data from its Paris operations center. Around 86,000 customer records were exported, including identity details, contact data, SIM replacement history, and internal escalation notes for high-value clients. The intrusion used valid credentials, a maintenance workstation on the technical floor, and an encrypted outbound transfer that blended into normal overnight traffic. Three suspects were brought in for questioning: Martin Lefevre, a former customer data analyst recently dismissed by Synapse; Antonin Morel, a field network subcontractor with after-hours technical access; and Claire Music, the night security guard on duty in the building during the incident.
key_facts: Unauthorized access to Synapse systems (Software platform of the school Telecom Paris) occurred between 02:13 and 03:01 on June 18, 2025
Approximately 86,000 customer records were exported from the CRM and SIM lifecycle tools
The stolen dataset included names, addresses, phone numbers, partial billing details, SIM swap history, and internal VIP escalation notes
The intrusion used valid credentials tied to a legacy service account that should have been disabled
A maintenance workstation on the seventh-floor technical area initiated the export and archive creation
The archive was transferred to an external cloud storage endpoint through an encrypted connection
Martin Lefevre was dismissed from Synapse on April 29, 2025 after a disciplinary dispute over unauthorized data handling
Lefevre previously worked on customer data exports and knew the structure of the CRM tables involved
Antonin Morel completed overnight network cabling work for a Synapse subcontractor the week before the breach
Morel had legitimate past access to the technical floor and was familiar with maintenance laptops and badge-controlled areas
Building badge logs show Claire Music patrolled the seventh floor at 02:21 and again at 02:43
Claire reported seeing a technician she assumed was authorized near the service corridor but did not escalate immediately
Endpoint logs show a USB-C external SSD was connected to the maintenance workstation at 02:26
A prepaid 4G router was used as a secondary network hop during the transfer
Martin and Antonin exchanged encrypted messages several times in the ten days preceding the breach
Antonin deposited 11,500 euros in cash two days after the incident with no clear business invoice to justify it
evidence: SIEM alert showing abnormal overnight export volume from Synapse CRM
VPN and workstation logs tied to the legacy service account
Archive creation logs from the seventh-floor maintenance workstation
USB device connection event at 02:26 on the compromised workstation
Encrypted outbound transfer to an external cloud storage endpoint
Encrypted message metadata linking Martin Lefevre and Antonin Morel before the breach
Cash deposit records linked to Antonin Morel after the incident
Security badge logs and corridor CCTV from Claire Music's patrol route
Unsigned overnight work order used to justify technical-floor presence
